events.text: Patient is a 60-year-old male of Hispanic/Latino race with an ECOG performance status of 1
At age 60, patient presented with diffuse lymphadenopathy, abdominal pain, early satiety, and 18-pound unintentional weight loss over six weeks with drenching night sweats
At age 60, excisional biopsy of a right cervical lymph node demonstrated diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, ICD-O site C77.9, histology code 9680/3

At age 60, pathology report showed large atypical lymphoid cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio and frequent apoptotic bodies, Ki-67 of 88 percent, immunohistochemistry positive for CD20, CD19, BCL6, MUM1, BCL2 strongly positive, negative for CD10, consistent with non-GCB (ABC) subtype by Hans algorithm
At age 60, flow cytometry demonstrated monoclonal B-cell population positive for CD20, CD19, BCL6, MUM1, negative for CD10, CD5

At age 60, FISH analysis showed MYC rearrangement positive, BCL2 rearrangement positive via t(14;18), BCL6 rearrangement negative; classified as double-hit high-grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 rearrangements

At age 60, laboratory workup revealed LDH markedly elevated at 682 U/L, CBC with WBC 14.2 with 8 percent circulating atypical lymphocytes, hemoglobin 10.8 g/dL, platelets 96, CMP with elevated creatinine 1.5 mg/dL, calcium 11.2 mg/dL, uric acid 11.8 mg/dL

At age 60, staging PET/CT showed extensive FDG-avid lymphadenopathy in cervical, mediastinal, retroperitoneal, mesenteric, and bilateral inguinal regions, largest retroperitoneal mass 13.1 cm with SUVmax 32.6, FDG-avid diffuse bone marrow infiltration and hepatic lesions consistent with extranodal involvement
At age 60, bone marrow biopsy showed 60 percent involvement by high-grade B-cell lymphoma with diffuse interstitial and paratrabecular infiltration
At age 60, CT-guided liver biopsy confirmed hepatic involvement by diffuse large B-cell lymphoma
At age 60, patient was staged as Ann Arbor Stage IV with B symptoms, IPI score 5 (high risk) based on age 60, elevated LDH, Stage IV, ECOG 1, and more than one extranodal site (bone marrow and liver)
At age 60, patient was admitted for initiation of cycle 1 of R-CHOP with aggressive tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis including rasburicase, allopurinol, IV fluids at 200 mL/hr, and cardiac monitoring

At age 60, during cycle 1, patient developed laboratory tumor lysis syndrome with potassium 5.8, phosphorus 6.9, uric acid 12.4, and LDH spike to 1,240 U/L, managed with rasburicase re-dosing, aggressive hydration, and electrolyte monitoring in the ICU

At age 60, on day 8 of cycle 1, patient developed grade 4 febrile neutropenia with ANC less than 100, temperature 104.1F, and hypotension requiring vasopressor support, treated with broad-spectrum antibiotics including meropenem and vancomycin
At age 60, patient received cycle 2 of R-CHOP with 25 percent dose reduction of all agents and mandatory pegfilgrastim, prophylactic levofloxacin and fluconazole added
At age 60, patient received cycle 3 of R-CHOP at reduced doses

At age 60, interim PET/CT after 3 cycles showed mixed response with reduction in cervical and inguinal nodes but persistent bulky retroperitoneal mass now 9.6 cm with SUVmax 21.4, Deauville score 5, and persistent hepatic and bone marrow FDG uptake
At age 60, interim PET showed refractory disease with double-hit biology, decision made to switch to salvage therapy after completing current cycle
At age 60, patient switched to salvage R-ICE: rituximab 375 mg/m2, ifosfamide 5000 mg/m2 with mesna, carboplatin AUC 5, etoposide 100 mg/m2 days 1 through 3
At age 60, after cycle 1 of R-ICE, patient developed grade 3 mucositis, grade 4 neutropenia, and grade 3 thrombocytopenia with platelet nadir 12,000 requiring multiple platelet transfusions
At age 60, patient received cycle 2 of R-ICE with dose reduction, complicated by prolonged cytopenias
At age 60, PET/CT after 2 cycles of R-ICE showed partial response with retroperitoneal mass decreased to 5.4 cm, SUVmax 11.8, Deauville score 5, hepatic lesions decreased but still FDG-avid
At age 61, patient completed cycle 3 of R-ICE salvage and underwent successful autologous stem cell collection yielding 4.8 million CD34+ cells/kg
At age 61, patient received BEAM conditioning chemotherapy followed by autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplant
At age 61, post-transplant course complicated by grade 4 mucositis, Clostridioides difficile colitis treated with oral vancomycin, and prolonged neutropenia with engraftment on day 16
At age 61, PET/CT at day 100 post-transplant showed significant residual retroperitoneal mass 3.1 cm with SUVmax 6.2, Deauville score 4, concerning for persistent disease post-transplant
At age 61, given persistent disease after auto-HSCT, patient was referred for evaluation of chimeric antigen receptor T-cell therapy clinical trial

scenario_blurb: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), germinal center and activated B-cell subtypes. Ann Arbor stage II-IV. Treated with R-CHOP. Include IPI scoring, PET-CT response assessment, some with relapse requiring R-ICE salvage and autologous stem cell transplant.
